text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: If Unimbɔti has life, it is through the other, and the other has life through Unimbɔti. There is no life except through the other.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: There is a great renown of his in the world, and there is a great renown of his on earth, and all the peoples will see his glory.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham took him away, and when he saw the place from afar, he prepared the sacrifice with the righteous men, and after he returned, he found abundant blessings.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: These are the names of the children of Israel who came to Egypt with Jacob, including Jacob and his descendants, who went there, and became the twelve tribes of the children of Israel, and they multiplied and became numerous.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: Jacob's child died in the village. He went to Unimbɔti yesterday and arrived at the town.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: The Unimbɔti gave peace for his life, and he gave life to the Unimbɔti for life. He did not give forgiveness for his sins.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: He did the work for God in the morning, he did the work for God in the evening, he did the work for God at noon, and on the third day he spoke the truth.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham left his land when he was called to go to a land that he would receive as an inheritance, and he went out not knowing where he was going, and his descendants inherited it, though he himself had no dwelling place, living in tents with Isaac and Jacob.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: When he sowed the seeds in the field, Jacob saw that the harvest was abundant, so he took the produce and ate it.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: He, Jacob, saw his son in Saŋŋu. He went to Unimbɔti to work on the farm.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: By Unimbɔti, he who created the sky and the earth, and he who created Unimbɔti himself. He is the one who gives life and breath.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: And the work and the people for the meal, and the children and the people for the meal, and the elders and the leaders of the people for the meal, do not do this in the village.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham journeyed with his servants to that place, arrived in the land of Canaan, built an altar there, offered sacrifices, called on the name of the Lord, and then moved his tent further east.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: So he served seven years for his wife, and they seemed to him but a few days because of the love he had for her. Then Jacob said to Laban, "Give me my wife now, for my time is completed, that I may go in to her." And Laban gave him his wife.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: Jacob was his elder brother. He named his younger son Unimbɔti, who was born to him.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: If Unimbɔti knows the child and the father, he also knows the father of Unimbɔti. He said that the child is the father's.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: The one who is unoon in the house of usal, the one who is kuwaŋkuluu in the house of usal, the one who is kun pée fù kitiŋ puee in the house of usal, they are the ones who truly speak the word.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham left there and went to Haran, taking his wife and all his wealth, and afterward returned to the Negev.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: Ŋyunti ŋun is the father of tiwaŋkuti, whose mother was Reumah, and Jacob's son is ndɔsɔmu, whose mother was Leah, the second, and the third is ndɔsɔmu whose mother was Rachel.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: Then Jacob gathered his sons and spoke to them. He said to them, "Gather together so that I may tell you what will happen to you in the days to come."

ref: We were out in the field, tying up bundles of wheat. Suddenly my bundle stood up, and your bundles gathered around and bowed down to it.”
text: M dámintì yii ti bí kusaau ki bōob̀ amɔpin, ní man yal yáatìi yúl̀, ní nimbi mɔpiŋŋi māntìi tú man mɔpindi ní ki lítì di-taapu.»
translated: They said that he had taken it to the village house, and that the people were looking for him, and that they would find him and bring him back to the town.

ref: Even before I had finished praying, Rebekah came by with a water jar on her shoulder. When she had filled the jar, I asked her for a drink.
text: Maa láá dākafì míǹ m-pɔbilin ki dóò, ní Reebeeka páaǹní ki túń u-baŋki u-gbigbil pu ki dɔ́miń ki sīntì dinyimbɔbilin ki lúń nnyim, ní m mēē-u yii: <M gbāam̀-si, pɔ̄-mi nnyim ḿ nyɔ̀.>
translated: Now Abraham had a servant in the city, and Rebecca went out to draw water, her jar on her hip, to the well to fill the jar and bring it back home, and he said to her: "Let me drink a little water from your jar."

ref: and this is the story about him. Noah was the only person who lived right and obeyed God.
text: Noowa nì u-biyaamu bɔti sɔ̀. Noowa nìn sá unimɔnti ní ki bí deedee u-yuntiŋu nib kansikin. U nìn nóó Unimbɔti mmɔntiim.
translated: My father's farm is fertile. My father has a house and also has a child named kansikin. He has Unimbɔti's land.

ref: where pure gold, rare perfumes, and precious stones are found.
text: Kitiŋ gbanti sindaariyau sá umɔnti ní, ní talaalu un bi yíì bideeliyoomee nì ataŋkpamɔnti ŋin bi yíì soowamee bí niin.
translated: Only the one who desires my life did not eat the bread or drink the water of the people.

ref: Jacob said to Laban, “The time is up, and I want to marry Rachel now!”
text: Niinee ní Jaakɔb bí Laaban yii: «M túń ditundi ki sāā m-yuntiŋu, an puee dū m-nimpuu ki tīī-mi ḿ cáá.»
translated: I have done according to your word, and now you have seen that my livestock is with me.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: God gave him life, and he gave his life for God. He did not give his children for money.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: When a person carries a burden during the day and at night, and when the burden is heavy during the day and at night, and when the burden is too much for the person during the day and at night, then death comes to the rescue.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place which he would later receive as an inheritance, and he left without knowing where he was going.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: These are the names of the sons of Jacob who were born to him in Beersheba, and the sons who were born to him in Paddan Aram, and he named the son who was born to him in Paddan Aram as Israel, and he had twelve sons, and these are the names of his sons who were born to him in Paddan Aram.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: Jacob met his wife Saŋŋu. He went to Unimbɔti, the land of Cílìń, to his father's house.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: If the child does not speak, it is not because of the child; the one who does not speak is the child himself. He who has no friend is the one without a friend.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham was the father of many nations, and he walked among the nations, becoming the ancestor of many peoples, walking in the land of Canaan, and serving God faithfully.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: When he had departed from that place, Jacob looked up and saw a well in the field, and behold, three flocks of sheep lying there beside it, because they used to water the flocks from that well.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: When Jacob had a dream, he saw the angel of God speaking to him in his father's house.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: If Unimbɔti gives something for life, he gives it for his own life. He does not give another thing or another matter.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: I have worked for rest, the elders have worked for rest, the children have worked for rest, my kin, the day of death comes inevitably.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham was his name, and he was the father of many nations, and he walked with God, and he spoke the truth, and he lived blamelessly before God always.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: When the time came for Isaac to die, he called his son Esau and said to him, "My son," and he answered, "Here I am." Then he said to him, "Now I am old; I do not know the day of my death."

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: When Jacob was born, he took his older brother by the heel. He was called the supplanter because he supplanted his brother twice.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: The Unimbɔti and the child are in the house, and the child is also in the Unimbɔti's house. The person is with the woman and the man.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: We do all our work with our hands, important tasks with our hands, preparing food with our hands, and also act with love.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham took his servants and all his possessions, went to the land of the Negev, built an altar there, and offered a burnt sacrifice.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: Then he took his sons and livestock and went to Beersheba, and Jacob sent his sons to take the rest of his livestock and his household, and he did the same with the rest.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: Jacob's son went to the river. He found the chief of the village there.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: In Unimbɔti, he has his word and in the house, he has within him the word of Unimbɔti here. He does the work with power here.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: There was a time when the child worked with hands, and the elders worked with hands, and also spoke words to the people with hands, Bi-kɔkɔ came forth with speech.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham arrived and was very afraid, and he spoke to them, saying, "In this place, I also told the people of the city, and they greatly feared God."

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: Then he said to his son Jacob, "Go to the field and bring me some game to eat," but Jacob was not willing to go to the field to hunt, so he went and brought back some game and cooked it for his father to eat.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: When Jacob's child died, he went to Unimbɔti to fetch the soul from the grave.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham was his servant, and he called upon his name, and he continued to bless him and he went out to do good.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: The man went to the land of his father to find a wife, and Jacob heard about his uncle's household, the daughters of the land, and went to his uncle's house.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: When Jacob's child died, he went to Unimbɔti to do something for nsanni.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: When Unimbɔti gave unil first, u-ba also gave first to Unimbɔti. U did not give uninja to unimpu.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: The one who created the heavens and the earth, the one who created the seas and the earth, the one who created all things and the earth, the elders declared it was good.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham rested much, and he went to his tent, and he prepared a meal and made peace offerings to God, and he gave thanks greatly.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: The descendants of Israel who came to Egypt, Jacob and his twelve sons, and their families, and they became the heads of the families.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: When Jacob took his children and followed his uncle, he went to the land of Unimbɔti to live in his father's house.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Ní Unimbɔti bí-u yii: «M gbìl̄, ama an kaa sá Isimaayeel bɔti ní m bíi sòoǹ, an sá a-nimpuu Saara di làá màal̀-si kibininjabiki. Á dāá pɔ̄-kì diyindi yii Isaaki. Úǹ nì u-naantiibi bin làá páanní boonee di làá fōō dipoondi din m pōoǹ dí nīn bí kí nīn cá jaanjaanee.
translated: In the book of Genesis, it says: "Abraham was distressed because they sent Ishmael away when he was still a child, and his mother Sarah was also very upset. But God said to him, 'Do not be distressed for the boy Isaac. He will become a great nation as well, for he is your descendant.'"

ref: Then the Lord God made clothes out of animal skins for the man and his wife.
text: Ní ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti dū tigban ki lōoǹ Adam nì u-nimpuu.
translated: The Lord God created the earth and the heavens, and Adam and his descendants.

ref: When the chief cook saw that Joseph had given a good meaning to the dream, he told Joseph, “I also had a dream. In it I was carrying three breadbaskets stacked on top of my head.
text: Bin pūul̀ ubɔti kpɔnɔbee kpiliu nín ká yii Jooseefi tùkū u-bɔɔ u-damintiiku taapu an ŋān̄ee, ní u mun bí Jooseefi yii: «Ma mun dámintì yii bikpɔnɔmɔntiibi tikabiti tita ŋɔ́kɔ́ tɔb pu ní m tú.
translated: When he came to his brothers at Shechem, they conspired against Joseph and said, "Here comes the dreamer! Let us throw him into a pit and say a wild animal devoured him. Then we will see what becomes of his dreams."

ref: God made two powerful lights, the brighter one to rule the day and the other to rule the night. He also made the stars.
text: Míǹ ní Unimbɔti ŋá iwalfikpaan ili: ŋkpaŋkpaan di sá ŋwìiń, kutaa yaa wúntèe, ŋú nīn bí ki ŋmíntí. Kiwaai di sá uŋmal, kunyeeuee, ú nīn bí ki ŋmíntí, ní ki ŋá iŋmalbijaa mun.
translated: The word of Unimbɔti created the world: the heavens he established firm, the earth he made to flourish, and he did not abandon them. His commandments he placed, holy and unchanging, and he will not violate his covenant forever.

ref: no grass or plants were growing anywhere. God had not yet sent any rain, and there was no one to work the land.
text: kisubiki báà kiba kaa nìn dáá nyáń kitiŋ pu, ní kumoou báà kubɔ mun kaa nìn dáá nyáń, kun puee ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti kaa nìn dáá yāntì utaa nìn̄. Unil mun nìn kpá kí kpɔ̀ tikpaal kitiŋ pu.
translated: Kisubiki did his work well, and Kumoou did his work well too, but Dindaan Unimbɔti did his work poorly. Unil did not complete his task well.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: God is the father of the universe, and the universe is the child of God. The universe is the son of the earth.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: When they begin the work for the person, when they continue the work for the person, when they finish the work for the person, then they will share the meal together.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham took his ram early in the morning, built an altar, arranged the firewood, and offered it as a burnt offering according to the word, and God looked upon it with favor.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: And he came to a certain place and stayed there overnight because the sun had set; and Jacob took one of the stones of the place and put it under his head, and lay down to sleep there.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: This is Jacob's wife Saŋŋu. She is the mother of Unimbɔti's children Tuuŋyi and Cílìń.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

translated: The way of the wicked is an abomination to the Lord, and the path of the wicked leads to death; the counsel of the wicked is far from salvation, but the prayer of the upright is His delight.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham went to Nimpuu and sowed seeds there, but the harvest was poor because the land was not fertile, yet he remained patient and eventually prospered greatly.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: When he left his homeland and all his possessions, Jacob went to the land of the East, and he found his relatives there.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: This is Jacob's son Simeon. He begot Levi, and his daughter Tamar he married.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: In Unimbɔti, he who has the word has the father, and he who has the father has the word in Unimbɔti. He who has a child has life.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: There is a saying: Bikoko will know the end of doing good work with hands and effort, and wicked work with hands and effort, and even false work with hands and effort.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham left his home and went to the land of Canaan, built an altar there to worship God, and gave offerings to the Lord.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: When Jacob left that place, he came to a certain town and stayed there; this was the house of his father, and he remained there for a while.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: "And Jacob came to the place and stayed there. He said to Unimbɔti, 'You are the one who will go there.'"

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: When Unimbɔti takes his child, he takes his father's child, and his father's child takes Unimbɔti. He took the chicken and the dog.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: When the sun rises, the bird sings on the branch, and the wind blows through the leaves, and the great one on the branch, and the child on the lap of the singer on the branch, the wind and the leaves, the heart of the sun is full of light.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham was very old, and he had a servant in his household, a chief servant who was in charge of all that he had. And he said to him, "Take your son, your only son..."

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: Then he named the place Laai Jotbe, and Jacob saw a well in the field, and he rolled the stone from the mouth of the well and watered the flock of his uncle Laban.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: When Jacob arrived at that place, he stayed there. He took a stone from that place and set it up as a pillar, and poured oil on its top.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: By Unimbɔti, he who created the sky and the earth, and he who created Unimbɔti himself. He is the one who gives life and breath.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: And the work and the people for the meal, and the children and the people for the meal, and the elders and the leaders of the people for the meal, do not do this in the village.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham journeyed with his servants to that place, arrived in the land of Canaan, built an altar there, offered sacrifices, called on the name of the Lord, and then moved his tent further east.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: So he served seven years for his wife, and they seemed to him but a few days because of the love he had for her. Then Jacob said to Laban, "Give me my wife now, for my time is completed, that I may go in to her." And Laban gave him his wife.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: Jacob was his elder brother. He named his younger son Unimbɔti, who was born to him.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: Ní Unimbɔti gives his life for unil, and he gives his life from Unimbɔti his Ní. He does not give uninja to unimpu his Ní.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: In the beginning, the word was empty and the earth; the word of darkness and the earth; the word of the flood and fire and the earth; on the day of judgment, the truth will be revealed.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham took his servant Mimmee and went to the land of Canaan, and he gathered his people and his servants and went to war against the kings and defeated them completely.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: And he said to his people, "Hear me, O house of Jacob," Jacob said to his people, "You who chose me, though you were not my people, I will be your father, and the people who chose me will listen to me."

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: Jacob spoke to his son. He sent Nimboti to bring his gift.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: In Unimbɔti, the child did not come to his house, he went to Unimbɔti's house. He said he did not go to the river.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.
translated: When they ate and were full, and when they drank and were satisfied, and when they went out and were content, then their hearts turned away from the word.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham was old and well advanced in years, and the Lord had blessed him with livestock, silver, and gold. He journeyed through the land of the Negev and dwelled near the city of Hebron. There he built an altar and worshiped the Lord repeatedly.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.

translated: When Unimbɔti gave unil first, u-ba also gave first to Unimbɔti. U did not give uninja to unimpu.

translated: Abraham rested much, and he went to his tent, and he prepared a meal and made peace offerings to God, and he gave thanks greatly.

translated: The descendants of Israel who came to Egypt, Jacob and his twelve sons, and their families, and they became the heads of the families.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.

text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: When Unimbɔti gives the word to the person, the person gives the word back to Unimbɔti. Then the person gives another word to the other.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

translated: He who acts like a person who acts and speaks, and like a wealthy person who acts and speaks, and like one who gives birth to a child who acts and speaks, he is indeed the one who speaks the truth.

translated: Abraham took his livestock and separated from his relatives, and he offered sacrifices and made a covenant with God, living in peace and prosperity.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.

translated: The Lord remembered his people and had compassion on them, and Jacob called his sons and gathered them together, his twelve sons, and spoke to them.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: God created humanity in his image; he created them in the image of God. And he created woman and man.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.

translated: Abraham took his departure from his country, from his kindred, and from his father's house, to go to the land of Canaan.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: And he called his sons and said to them, "Gather together, that I may tell you what will happen to you in the future. Jacob blessed his sons, each according to his own blessing, with the blessings of the twelve tribes."

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: This is the word that Jacob spoke. He said that King Unimbɔti came to the village.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: God gave his life for us, and he gave me the life of God. Through faith and love.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

translated: When you take a cow to the farm and return, and bring back the cow from the farm, and go to the farm to fetch the cow and return, the one who comes with words will understand the meaning.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.

translated: Abraham rose early in the morning, took his servant, prepared the donkey, took the two young men with him, and they journeyed for three days to the place he had been commanded, and on the third day he saw the place from afar.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: So Jacob fled to Padan Aram because of his brother Esau, and Jacob served Laban for seven years to marry Rachel, but Laban gave him Leah his older sister instead, and Jacob also worked another seven years for her.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.

translated: Jacob spoke to his servant. He said to the Lord's servant, "Come to his people."

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: If Unimbɔti has a word and a way, he has it through Unimbɔti. He does not have another way or substitute.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

translated: I have done the work of the child, the work of the elder, and the work of the woman, and this has caused me to become tired.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham took his child and went to Canaan, and he received the chosen people and the blessing of the land given to him, and the covenant to keep forever.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.

translated: And he went out from that place and came to a certain place and stayed there overnight, for the sun had set; and Jacob took one of the stones of the place and put it under his head and lay down in that place to sleep, and he dreamed that there was a ladder set up on the earth.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.

text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: If Unimbɔti gives what he eats, he gives what he eats of Unimbɔti. He gives both taking and receiving.

translated: He left from there and went to a place called Haran. Jacob stayed in Haran with Laban for twenty years and he left Haran with his family.

translated: For Unimbɔti gives to the one who asks, and to the one who seeks, Unimbɔti gives. And he does not withhold mercy from those who ask.

translated: Abraham departed from Mamre, went to see all the land of the Negev, and dwelled in Egypt, and he called his servants and took his wife and gave her to Pharaoh for many gifts.

translated: In Unimbɔti, he gives life to the weary and rest to those who are weary in Unimbɔti. He gives good things to those who ask.

translated: Abraham took the wood for the burnt offering, cut the animal into pieces, arranged the pieces opposite each other, and the smoke ascended as a fragrant offering to God.

text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: If Unimbɔti knows the way, he will know the way through Unimbɔti. He does not know the enemy and the helper.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

translated: Abraham was old and advanced in age, and he had no child; thus, he considered making his servant, who was in his house, his heir, as he had greatly prospered.

translated: Abraham took his servant with him, and he said to him, "Let us go on a journey," and he prepared the donkeys and took provisions for the journey, and he took silver to buy many things.

translated: Jacob lived in the land of Canaan. He dwelled in the house of God for many days.

translated: The Unimbɔti has his word and the Father's, and he has his word and the Father's. He is a child and has power.

translated: Abraham left Haran, taking his wife Sarai, his nephew Lot, and all their possessions, and they settled in the land of Canaan, but later returned to Haran again.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.

translated: When the shepherds had gathered there by the well, Jacob watered the flocks of Laban his mother's brother, and he loved Rachel and agreed to work for Laban seven years, but Laban deceived him and gave him Leah instead.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.

translated: This is Jacob, his firstborn was Saŋgu. He begot Unimbɔti, the second son, who went to Nsanni.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: In God there is life and the word of life, and he has in him the word of God. He who has eternal life and truth in him.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

translated: When he did the work with care, and completed the task with care, and began the new work with care, then he received what was due to him.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.

translated: Abraham was faithful to his Lord, accepted His covenant, received blessings for his descendants, proclaimed the word, and repeatedly promised the land.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.

translated: These are the generations of Isaac, the son of Abraham, who begot Jacob; and Jacob had twelve sons, whose names are listed here, and all the descendants of Jacob who went to Egypt numbered seventy.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.

translated: When Jacob saw his daughter, he took her. He went to Unimbɔti and buried her there.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.

translated: In the beginning, God created the heavens and the earth. And the earth was created by God. Then he created animals and plants.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

translated: He created the earth on the water, and the deep within the earth, and the dust of the earth from the soil, and the Creator made the heavens.

translated: Abraham said to him, "Take your hand and bring him to the high place to sacrifice him, and he spoke to him with great reverence."

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.

translated: When the time came for the twins to be born, Jacob struggled with Esau in the womb, and the Lord said to her, "Two nations are in your womb, and the older will serve the younger."

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: Then Jacob spoke to them the words of his mouth. He made the work of the young child of his servant Nsanni.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: Ní Unimbɔti gives his service to him, and he gives his service to Ní Unimbɔti. He does not forgive sins, but gives mercy.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

translated: There is a time for the generation to walk in the way of the world, and for the leaders to walk in the way of the world, and for those who care for the children to walk in the way of the world, but the road walked is knowledge.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.

translated: Abraham journeyed to the Negev, and there was a famine in the land, so he went down to Egypt to stay there for a while.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.

translated: And he sent him from the valley of Hebron, and when he came to Shechem, and they sent him again to his brothers.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.

translated: Jacob was his younger brother. He also had a child named Unimbɔti, who fathered Tuuŋyi, and his child was Nsanni.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.
translated: God knows and sees them, and they know and see God. He knows their hearts and their thoughts.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

translated: Our Father who is in heaven, hallowed be your name, your kingdom come, your will be done on earth as it is in heaven, give us today our daily bread, and forgive us our debts as we also have forgiven our debtors, and do not lead us into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.

translated: Abraham spoke plainly, that he made a covenant to circumcise, so that his descendants would be scattered among the nations but he would guide them, and they would truly serve God forever.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: And it came to pass when Isaac became old and his eyes were very weak from seeing, that Jacob his son came to him to receive the blessing he would give to his firstborn son Esau, and he ate of the blessing that his father had prepared.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: Jacob took his staff. He saw Unimbɔti approaching, his eyes fixed on the house.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.

translated: A person's name is known through their deeds, and their deeds are known through their name. This is how the two are known.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

translated: In the morning, when they see you; in the afternoon, when they see you; and in the evening, when they see you, then they will know the truth.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.

translated: Abraham was very old, and his days were many, and he had become very rich in silver and gold, and his servants and livestock had multiplied greatly, and he was very honored among the people.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.

translated: In Unimbɔti, he has his word and in the house, he has within him the word of Unimbɔti here. He does the work with power here.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

translated: There was a time when the child worked with hands, and the elders worked with hands, and also spoke words to the people with hands, Bi-kɔkɔ came forth with speech.

translated: Abraham arrived and was very afraid, and he spoke to them, saying, "In this place, I also told the people of the city, and they greatly feared God."

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.

translated: Then he said to his son Jacob, "Go to the field and bring me some game to eat," but Jacob was not willing to go to the field to hunt, so he went and brought back some game and cooked it for his father to eat.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.

translated: When Jacob's child died, he went to Unimbɔti to fetch the soul from the grave.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.

translated: Abraham left his homeland, and he went to a place called Haran, and he took all his possessions with him and gave them to his descendants, and he came to the land that I will show him.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
